11/01/26, 22:58 - gladwin: why you mute
11/01/26, 22:59 - gladwin: There is no direct verse in the Bible that explicitly states a bride and groom should have opposite personalities (e.g., an introvert must marry an extrovert).

However, there is a very famous biblical concept—often mistranslated or oversimplified—that supports the idea of complementary differences rather than identical sameness.
11/01/26, 22:59 - Divs ❤️: u searchh
11/01/26, 22:59 - gladwin: yeahhh
11/01/26, 22:59 - gladwin: got it
11/01/26, 22:59 - gladwin: nothing like that
11/01/26, 22:59 - gladwin: samadhanam ayileeeee
11/01/26, 22:59 - gladwin: 2. The "Similar" Requirement: 2 Corinthians 6:14
While the Bible allows for different personalities, it gives a strict warning against being opposite in values and faith.

"Do not be unequally yoked together with unbelievers. For what fellowship has righteousness with lawlessness?" (2 Corinthians 6:14)

The deeper meaning: In farming, a "yoke" connects two oxen so they can pull a plow.

If you yoke a tall ox with a short ox (unequal physical traits), they will walk in circles and hurt each other.

The Bible demands that a couple be "equally yoked" spiritually and morally.

Conclusion: You can have opposite personalities (quiet vs. loud), but you must have the same foundational direction and values.
11/01/26, 23:00 - gladwin: Trait,Biblical View,Reference
Values / Faith,Must be the Same,2 Corinthians 6:14 (Equally yoked)
Life Purpose,Must be Aligned,"Amos 3:3 (""Can two walk together, except they be agreed?"")"
Personality,Can be Different,"Genesis 2:18 (A helper ""opposite"" or corresponding to him)"
Strengths,Should Complement,Ecclesiastes 4:9 (Two are better than one; they have a good return for their labor)

28/04/26, 23:48 - gladwin: # Everything I learned in this chat — full handover

Here's the complete knowledge base I built up across our conversation, organised so you (or anyone helping you) can pick up exactly where we left off.

---

## 1. The exam itself

**AIISH BASLP entrance — All India Institute of Speech and Hearing, Mysuru**

- **Course:** Bachelor of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology (4-year UG, includes 1 year internship)
- **Conducted by:** AIISH Mysore (autonomous institute under Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of India)
- **Application portal:** aiish.applyadmission.co.in
- **Official site:** aiishmysore.in
- **Eligibility (your case):** 10+2 with min 50% in Science stream (PCB/PCM), at least 3 of Physics/Chemistry/Biology/Maths
- **Age limit:** Not more than 35 years
- **Your exam date:** 26 May 2026

### Paper format (confirmed from 2024 PCB Shift 1 paper)
- **150 MCQs in 150 minutes** (1 mark each)
- **3 sections:** Physics 50 + Chemistry 50 + Biology 50
- **Computer-based test (CBT)**
- **No negative marking visible on the 2024 BASLP paper** (different from MSc papers which have 25% negative marking — verify on your admit card)
- **Single-day exam, single shift** for BASLP

### What's NOT in the paper (important — I got this wrong initially)
- ❌ No English / Reading Comprehension section
- ❌ No General Knowledge / Current Affairs
- ❌ No Logical Reasoning / Aptitude
- ❌ No separate Maths section
- ❌ No SLP-specific content (no phonetics, IPA, ear anatomy detail, linguistics, RPWD Act trivia)

It's effectively a **NEET-clone for Class 12 PCB students.**

---

## 2. Source confusion to be aware of

The URL you originally shared (`revised-msc-slp-syllabus-2025-26.pdf`) is the **MSc SLP postgraduate course curriculum** — what students study *after* getting in. Not relevant to your BASLP entrance prep. The BASLP entrance follows **CBSE 11th & 12th NCERT (rationalised 2025-26)**.

---

## 3. Real question-weight distribution (from 2024 paper)

This is the single most important piece of information for your prep.

### Physics (50 Qs)
| Block | Qs | Chapters |
|---|---|---|
| **Mechanics** | ~12 | Cls 11 Ch 1-7 |
| **Electromagnetism** | ~12 | Cls 12 Ch 1-7 |
| Properties of matter / Thermo / KTG / SHM / Waves | ~9 | Cls 11 Ch 8-14 |
| Optics + EM waves | ~6 | Cls 12 Ch 8-10 |
| Modern physics (photoelectric, atoms, nuclei, dual nature) | ~5 | Cls 12 Ch 11-13 |
| Electronic devices | ~1 | Cls 12 Ch 14 |

**Mechanics and Electromagnetism are tied as the heaviest blocks.**

### Chemistry (50 Qs)
| Block | Qs | Chapters |
|---|---|---|
| **Organic chemistry (single biggest block)** | ~17 | Cls 11 Ch 8-9 + Cls 12 Ch 6-9 |
| Cls 11 Physical (atomic, bonding, thermo, equilibrium, redox) | ~13 | Cls 11 Ch 1-7 |
| Cls 12 Physical (solutions, electrochem, kinetics) | ~10 | Cls 12 Ch 1-3 |
| Inorganic (d/f-block, coordination) | ~4 | Cls 12 Ch 4-5 |
| Biomolecules | ~3 | Cls 12 Ch 10 |

### Biology (50 Qs) — Class 12 dominates
| Block | Qs | Chapters |
|---|---|---|
| **Biotechnology (highest-yield Bio block)** | ~9 | Cls 12 Ch 9-10 |
| Genetics + Molecular Biology | ~8 | Cls 12 Ch 4-5 |
| Cls 11 Human Physiology | ~11 | Cls 11 Ch 14-19 |
| Reproduction | ~6 | Cls 12 Ch 1-3 |
| Evolution + Health + Microbes | ~6 | Cls 12 Ch 6-8 |
| Diversity + Structural + Cell (Cls 11) | ~6 | Cls 11 Ch 1-10 |
| Ecology + Biodiversity | ~4 | Cls 12 Ch 11-13 |

**Class 12 Bio outweighs Class 11 Bio by roughly 2:1.**

---

## 4. Specific high-yield topics from the 2024 paper (memorise these)

### Physics — recurring question types
- **Kinematics with derivatives:** x(t) given as polynomial, find v=dx/dt, a=d²x/dt², zero-crossings
- **Vector dot product:** angle between (i+j) and (i-j); when |A+B| = |A|, what does it imply
- **Rotational mechanics (3 Qs):** radius of gyration of disc (R/√2), KE of rotating mass, angular acceleration in uniform rotation
- **Network resistors / EMF / internal resistance** — bring out Kirchhoff's laws fluently
- **Magnetic moment ratio** of square loop vs circular loop with same wire length (answer: π:4)
- **Energy in inductor:** U = ½LI² → halving current gives U/4
- **EM spectrum order** by wavelength: microwaves > visible > X-rays
- **Telescope:** M = f₀/fₑ; length = f₀ + fₑ
- **Lens cut along principal axis:** focal length doubles (15 cm → 30 cm)
- **Diffraction with violet vs red:** fringe width decreases with smaller λ
- **Photoelectric:** threshold wavelength inversely proportional to work function (φ₀/4 → 4λ₀)
- **H-atom KE in nth state:** Eₙ = -13.6/n² eV; KE = -E
- **Rectifier:** half-wave output = input freq (50 Hz); full-wave = 2× input (100 Hz)

### Chemistry — recurring question types
- **Mole concept:** molecules in M×V×Nₐ
- **Empirical formula** from %C, %H, %O analysis
- **Quantum numbers:** l can be 0 to (n-1); l=4 with n=4 is impermissible
- **Bohr transitions:** least energetic photon = smallest ΔE = highest n's (n=6 → n=5)
- **Electron gain enthalpy:** Cl > F > Br > I (Cl is highest, not F!)
- **VSEPR bond angles:** sp³ = 109°, sp² = 120°, sp = 180°
- **Lattice energy & melting point** of alkali halides: NaCl is highest among LiCl/NaCl/KCl/RbCl
- **pH of 10⁻⁸ HCl:** between 6 and 7 (water autoionisation matters at this dilution)
- **Solubility from Ksp:** for MX type S=√Ksp; for MX₂ type S=∛(Ksp/4); compare orders
- **Tautomerism:** needs α-H + carbonyl → 2-pentanone yes, phenol no
- **Calcium carbide + water → acetylene** (CaC₂ + 2H₂O → Ca(OH)₂ + C₂H₂)
- **Ozonolysis → formaldehyde** confirms vinyl group (CH₂=CH-)
- **Aldol product from CH₃CHO** → β-hydroxy aldehyde → crotonaldehyde
- **Iodoform test** positive: CH₃CHO and CH₃CH(OH)CH₃ (any compound giving CH₃CO- after oxidation)
- **C–Cl bond in chlorobenzene** is shorter and stronger than in CH₃Cl (resonance)
- **Highest pKa:** Ethanol > Phenol > o-Nitrophenol (ethanol is least acidic)
- **Gabriel synthesis** works only for primary aliphatic amines (not aryl amines like aniline)
- **Reduction of nitro to amine:** Fe/HCl is preferred reagent
- **Charge for MnO₄⁻ → MnO₂ reduction:** 3F (Mn⁷⁺ → Mn⁴⁺ = 3 electrons)
- **First-order 90% completion:** ~3.3 × half-life
- **EMF–K relation:** E°cell = (0.0591/n) log K; for 1-electron 0.591V → K = 10¹⁰
- **In voltaic cell:** oxidation at anode, reduction at cathode (electrolytic cell — opposite for sign labels)
- **Gum** = polysaccharide
- **Casein** = simple protein

### Biology — recurring question types
- **Taxonomic hierarchy:** Phylum is the highest among Order/Class/Phylum/Genus
- **Oxyhaemoglobin dissociation:** lower temperature does NOT cause dissociation (it favours binding)
- **Selaginella** has compact strobili (heterosporous pteridophyte)
- **Nereis** osmoregulates via **nephridia**
- **Monoadelphous stamen** = China rose (single bundle)
- **PCT** maintains pH/HCO₃ and ionic balance
- **ADH** prevents diuresis
- **Joint disorder mismatch:** Muscular dystrophy ≠ osteoporosis description (osteoporosis = age-related bone mass loss)
- **Joints:** Ball & socket = humerus-pectoral; Hinge = knee; Pivot = atlas-axis; Gliding = carpals; Saddle = thumb
- **Medulla oblongata** controls gastric secretion
- **PTH** maintains calcium homeostasis
- **Steroid hormones:** Cortisol, Testosterone, Estradiol, Progesterone (NOT insulin/thyroid/adrenaline)
- **Filiform apparatus** in synergids guides pollen tube
- **Haploid cells in embryo sac:** antipodals + egg cell (synergids also haploid; integuments and nucellus are diploid)
- **Oogenesis initiates in embryonic stage** (foetal life)
- **Selectable marker:** ampR (antibiotic resistance gene)
- **DNA stained with ethidium bromide** for UV visualisation
- **cryIAb** controls corn borer; **cryIAc** controls cotton bollworm
- **α-1-antitrypsin** treats emphysema
- **Insulin pro-hormone** has C-peptide (removed during maturation)
- **Earthworm** respires only through body surface (cutaneous)
- **Blood group O** has BOTH anti-A and anti-B antibodies in plasma
- **ANF** released by atrial stretch (high atrial blood flow)
- **GFR fall** activates JG cells → renin (NOT aldosterone directly)
- **Muscle contraction:** A-band stays SAME width (does NOT widen) — H-zone shrinks, I-band shrinks, sarcomere shortens
- **Humoral immunity** = B-cells; Cell-mediated = T-cells
- **Cyclosporin A** from *Trichoderma polysporum* (immunosuppressant)
- **Spliceosomes** absent in bacteria (no introns to splice)
- **Bt toxin** binds midgut epithelial cells; activated by alkaline (not acidic) pH
- **Lichens** = primary succession pioneer on bare rock
- ***Anabaena*** with Azolla; ***Azotobacter*** = free-living N-fixer
- **NPP = GPP − Plant respiration**
- **Western Ghats** = Indian biodiversity hotspot
- **Down syndrome** = trisomy 21
- **Hershey-Chase** used ³⁵S (protein) + ³²P (DNA)
- **snRNA** transcribed by RNA Polymerase III
- ***i*-gene** in lac operon codes for repressor protein
- ***Homo habilis*** cranial capacity: 650-800 cc
- **Corpus luteum / progesterone** maintains endometrium (not pregnancy directly)
- **SCID** is NOT an IVF technique (it's a disease — others ZIFT/ICSI/IUT are IVF)
- **LNG-20** = hormonal IUD (only hormonal method among CuT/condom/cervical cap)
- **Hardy-Weinberg:** affected by gene flow, drift, mutation, recombination, natural selection (NOT stable across generations when these act)
- **Industrial melanism** = natural selection example
- **Interferons** = cytokine barrier (innate immunity)

---

## 5. NCERT 2025-26 rationalised syllabus — official chapter list

### Class 11 Biology (5 units, ~22 chapters; deletions noted)
- **Unit I — Diversity:** Ch 1 Living World, Ch 2 Biological Classification, Ch 3 Plant Kingdom, Ch 4 Animal Kingdom
- **Unit II — Structural Organisation:** Ch 5 Morphology of Flowering Plants, Ch 6 Anatomy of Flowering Plants, Ch 7 Structural Organisation in Animals
- **Unit III — Cell:** Ch 8 Cell Theory, Ch 9 Biomolecules, Ch 10 Cell Cycle & Cell Division
- **Unit IV — Plant Physiology:** Ch 11 Photosynthesis, Ch 12 Respiration in Plants, Ch 13 Plant Growth & Development *(Transport in Plants and Mineral Nutrition DELETED)*
- **Unit V — Human Physiology:** Ch 14 Breathing & Exchange of Gases, Ch 15 Body Fluids & Circulation, Ch 16 Excretion, Ch 17 Locomotion & Movement, Ch 18 Neural Control, Ch 19 Chemical Coordination *(Digestion & Absorption is now formative-only — not in summative exam)*

### Class 12 Biology (5 units, 13 chapters)
- **Unit VI — Reproduction:** Ch 1 Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Ch 2 Human Reproduction, Ch 3 Reproductive Health
- **Unit VII — Genetics & Evolution:** Ch 4 Inheritance & Variation, Ch 5 Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Ch 6 Evolution
- **Unit VIII — Biology in Human Welfare:** Ch 7 Human Health & Disease, Ch 8 Microbes in Human Welfare
- **Unit IX — Biotechnology:** Ch 9 Principles & Processes, Ch 10 Applications
- **Unit X — Ecology:** Ch 11 Organisms & Populations, Ch 12 Ecosystem, Ch 13 Biodiversity & Conservation

### Class 11 Physics (10 units)
Ch 1 Units & Measurements, Ch 2-3 Kinematics, Ch 4 Laws of Motion, Ch 5 Work-Energy-Power, Ch 6 System of Particles & Rotation, Ch 7 Gravitation, Ch 8 Mechanical Properties of Solids, Ch 9 Fluids, Ch 10 Thermal Properties, Ch 11 Thermodynamics, Ch 12 Kinetic Theory, Ch 13 Oscillations, Ch 14 Waves

### Class 12 Physics (9 units)
Ch 1-2 Electrostatics, Ch 3 Current Electricity, Ch 4 Magnetic Effects, Ch 5 Magnetism & Matter, Ch 6 EMI, Ch 7 AC, Ch 8 EM Waves, Ch 9 Ray Optics, Ch 10 Wave Optics, Ch 11 Dual Nature, Ch 12 Atoms, Ch 13 Nuclei, Ch 14 Semiconductors

### Class 11 Chemistry (9 chapters after rationalisation)
Ch 1 Some Basic Concepts, Ch 2 Atomic Structure, Ch 3 Periodic Classification, Ch 4 Chemical Bonding, Ch 5 Thermodynamics, Ch 6 Equilibrium, Ch 7 Redox, Ch 8 Organic Chemistry Basic Principles, Ch 9 Hydrocarbons

### Class 12 Chemistry (10 chapters)
Ch 1 Solutions, Ch 2 Electrochemistry, Ch 3 Chemical Kinetics, Ch 4 d & f Block, Ch 5 Coordination Compounds, Ch 6 Haloalkanes & Haloarenes, Ch 7 Alcohols/Phenols/Ethers, Ch 8 Aldehydes/Ketones/Carboxylic Acids, Ch 9 Amines, Ch 10 Biomolecules

## 10. The single most useful sentence I can leave you with

**The 2024 paper showed that the actual exam looks much more like NEET than like a "speech & hearing entrance" — so prepping with NEET-level NCERT MCQs (especially mechanics, electromagnetism, organic chemistry, molecular biology, and biotech) is exactly the right strategy.**

Good luck on 26 May. You have 28 days. Day 1 starts today.
